assertions.description: INCORRECT: The OPamp-adding schematic is a summing/adding amplifier: three resistors (R1, R2, R3) all connect to the inverting input of U2, and R3 also serves as the feedback resistor. The analyzer detects it as a plain 'inverting' configuration using only R1 and R3 (input and feedback), missing that R2 is a second input resistor feeding the same summing node. A summing amplifier is a distinct topology (output is weighted sum of inputs) that warrants its own 'summing_inverting' or 'summing' configuration label and gain formula.
